$schema: Ralph Phase 1 backlog: feature list with passes, scope, dependencies.

features.feature: Phase 1 MVP: Player + Target + Pet frames
features.scope: Core frames, layout from defaultlayout.lua, health/power, no options/media.
modules/cast.lua, UnitCastingInfo/UnitChannelInfo, detached bar, spark, Blizzard bar hide.
features.notes: TBC APIs only; hidden.cast only hides Blizzard bar.

features.scope: modules/health.lua UpdateAura using UnitDebuff when C_UnitAuras absent.
modules/auras.lua, UnitBuff/UnitDebuff, anchors, borders, OmniCC, tooltips.
features.notes: ResolveAnchorPoints in defaultlayout; auras merged for skipped units.

features.feature: Level number color by level differential
features.scope: Color the level number on unit frames (e.g. target) by differential to player level: red (much higher), yellow (same-ish), green (slightly lower), grey (trivial/no XP). Like quest log level coloring. TBC: UnitLevel(unit), player level; apply color to the [level] tag or equivalent text.
features.notes: Quest-style: red/yellow/green/grey by level diff. Level param configured in modules/defaultlayout.lua (e.g. target: [level( )][perpp] line 872).

features.scope: Adjust the `[classification]` tag in `modules/tags.lua` so it returns short flags instead of words: `R` for rare, `+` for elite, `R+` for rare elite, and (optionally) a symbol for worldboss. Ensure defaultlayout usages like `[classification( )][perpp]` show these symbols before percentage.
features.notes: Request based on target UI text: e.g. rare creature shows `R` before `perpp` in `[classification( )][perpp]`.

features.feature: Live health/power text via unit events
features.scope: Ensure tags like [curmaxhp], [curhp], [curmaxpp], and [perpp] update immediately when health or power change, without requiring a full frame reload. Implemented by wiring Tags:Register to register UNIT_HEALTH/UNIT_MAXHEALTH/UNIT_CONNECTION and UNIT_POWER_FREQUENT/UNIT_MAXPOWER/UNIT_DISPLAYPOWER to fontString:UpdateTags for any text that references those tags.
features.notes: Implemented in modules/tags.lua; minimal per-event work (UnitHealth/UnitPower reads + SetText). Documented as TBC-safe and within RULES performance expectations.

features.feature: Health/power text layout and color polish
features.scope: Polish health and power text on player/target: center [perhp] and [perpp] on their respective bars, abbreviate large health values (e.g. 12345 -> 12.3k) in [curhp]/[curmaxhp], and add colored percentage tags ([perhpclass], [perppclass], [perpppowercolor]) using class and power colors for readability.
features.notes: Implemented in modules/tags.lua and modules/defaultlayout.lua; purely visual polish, not essential to minimal functionality.

features.scope: Brainstorm and list cleanup areas (dead code, retail-only paths, duplication, comments), then group and rank by impact. No code changes yet.
features.notes: Done: docs/cleanup-plan.md. Design-only; refine into smaller slices before implementation.

features.scope: Auras module rewrite: Button frames with SetScale for self-cast debuffs (1.30x), caster detection via sourceUnit/playerUnits lookup, bottom-aligned positioning so mixed-scale icons share a baseline, debuff-type-colored borders. Supports both C_UnitAuras and UnitAura fallback paths.
features.notes: Key learnings: SetScale only works on Button frames (not Frame); isFromPlayerOrPlayerPet is true for all player-type casters, must use sourceUnit directly; bottom-align via BOTTOMLEFT/BOTTOMRIGHT anchoring.

features.scope: Enable player buffs with a hardcoded whitelist: only whitelisted buffs are shown. Whitelist is a named Lua table `playerBuffWhitelist` at the top of LoadDefaultLayout in defaultlayout.lua, passed as `whitelist = playerBuffWhitelist` in the player buffs config. auras.lua checks spellId (preferred) then name; no whitelist = show all. whitelist = {} in AceDB defaults prevents verifyTable from stripping it. Lazy container creation in updateAuraList ensures the buffs container exists even if buffs were enabled after OnEnable ran.
features.notes: Player frame only. Uses spell ID keys for robustness (e.g. [16870] = true for Clearcasting). buildContainer now defined before updateAuraList to avoid Lua nil-global trap.

features.feature: Party frame fade when member is out of range
features.scope: Detect when a party member is out of range and fade their unit frame to a lower alpha (e.g. 0.4). Poll or use UNIT_FLAGS/UNIT_CONNECTION events to check `UnitInRange(unit)` or `CheckInteractDistance`. Restore full alpha when back in range. Player frame unaffected. Ideally reuse or extend any existing combat fader alpha infrastructure.
features.notes: TBC API: `UnitInRange(unit)` returns true/false for party1–party4. May need a periodic OnUpdate poll since there is no dedicated range-change event in TBC.

features.scope: Add optional `borderColor = {r, g, b, a}` field to castBar config in defaultlayout.lua for each unit. Modify `setCastBarBorderColor` in modules/cast.lua to read `config.borderColor` when present, falling back to the current hardcoded dark grey (0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 1). No other changes needed.
features.notes: Motivated by wanting the target cast bar a different color from the player. Config change only in defaultlayout.lua once cast.lua supports it.

features.feature: CLI syntax validation in build.sh (luac + TOC check)
features.scope:
Two unconditional checks added to build.sh that run before any file is copied, so the agent gets a fast, reliable fail signal it can self-correct against without loading WoW.

1. luac -p syntax check: for every .lua file in modules/, localization/, libs/ root, and ShadowedUnitFrames.lua, run `luac -p <file>`. Collect all failures and print them, then exit 1 if any failed. No luacheck install required — just the `lua` package (sudo apt install lua5.1 or lua5.4). Runs on source files before the copy step.

2. TOC file-existence check: parse ShadowedUnitFrames.toc, extract every line that ends in .lua or .xml (skip ## comments and blank lines), resolve each path relative to the repo root, and assert the file exists. Print each missing path and exit 1 if any are absent.

Both checks must: (a) print a clear, actionable error line per failure, (b) exit non-zero so `set -e` stops the build, and (c) run before the rm -rf $BUILD_DIR step so a failed check never leaves a partial build. Update RULES.md section 5 (Test Before Push) to note that `./build/build.sh` is the agentic self-correction command and passes only when both checks are green.
features.notes: Motivation: agentic self-correction loop needs at least one CLI-runnable validation signal. luac -p catches syntax errors (missing `end`, bad string escapes, etc.) without needing any WoW-specific stubs. TOC check catches the most common deploy-breaking mistake (file added to TOC but not on disk, or renamed without updating TOC). luacheck (already in build.sh) remains as an optional deeper lint on top of these two hard checks.

features.feature: WoW TBC API validation: globals whitelist + stub load test
features.scope:
Two complementary checks that validate WoW-specific API usage without running the game client. Neither replaces in-game testing but both give the agent a CLI signal before WoW is ever launched.

**Layer 1 — luacheck globals whitelist (.luacheckrc)**
Create a `.luacheckrc` in the repo root. Set `std = 'none'` so luacheck assumes no built-in globals. Define a `globals` list containing every WoW TBC Anniversary API the addon is allowed to call (UnitHealth, UnitAura, UnitBuff, UnitDebuff, UnitCastingInfo, UnitChannelInfo, UnitClass, UnitExists, UnitIsAFK, UnitInRange, IsSpellInRange, CreateFrame, UIParent, GameTooltip, CopyTable, MAX_PARTY_MEMBERS, MAX_RAID_MEMBERS, MAX_BOSS_FRAMES, SlashCmdList, SLASH_*, etc.). Also list WoW-injected addon globals (ShadowUF, LibStub). Explicitly **exclude** known retail-only APIs (GetSpecialization, C_UnitAuras, AuraUtil, BackdropTemplateMixin) — luacheck will flag any usage as an undefined global. Update build.sh so this config is used when luacheck runs; make luacheck required (not optional) if it is installed in the dev environment, otherwise document install step. The `.luacheckrc` globals list becomes the machine-readable source of truth for the TBC API whitelist, cross-referencing docs/API_REFERENCE.md.

**Layer 2 — stub-based load test (test/stubs.lua + test/run.lua)**
Create `test/stubs.lua` that defines no-op versions of every WoW API and global constant the addon touches at load time: CreateFrame returns a table with no-op methods (SetPoint, SetSize, SetScript, RegisterEvent, RegisterUnitEvent, Show, Hide, etc.), UnitHealth/UnitClass/etc. return safe defaults, LibStub is a minimal stub, AceDB-3.0 returns an object with RegisterCallback no-op. Create `test/run.lua` that dofile()s stubs then dofile()s each source module in TOC order. Run with `lua test/run.lua`; any load-time nil-index or undefined-global error fails the test. Add this to build.sh as a step after the syntax check and before the file copy. This catches a class of errors that already appear in RULES.md section 9 (e.g. db.global nil because global table missing from AceDB defaults, buildContainer defined after its first call site) — errors that currently only surface after loading WoW.

Both checks must exit non-zero with a clear per-error message. Neither check validates in-game runtime behavior (event flow, frame rendering) — that remains a manual WoW test.
features.notes: Motivation: RULES.md section 8 lists retail-only APIs to avoid (GetSpecialization, C_UnitAuras, AuraUtil). Currently no tooling enforces this; an agent can introduce a retail API and only discover the error after loading WoW. Layer 1 makes the whitelist machine-checkable. Layer 2 catches load-time errors that already appear in RULES.md section 9 gotchas (db.global nil, buildContainer ordering, etc.). Known limitation: stubs cannot replicate WoW's OOP frame system or event dispatch — only load-time globals and call paths are tested.

features.scope: Placeholder for future cleanup work after plan is ready. Will be replaced by smaller, concrete items (e.g. remove unused module X, simplify units.lua Y).
features.notes: Do not use directly; split into concrete features via grooming.
